references.label: NHS App web integration guidance
references.borrowed: Header suppression, site-link constraints, and webview limitations reinforce that embedded route presentation must not fork host-owned behavior.
references.rejected: No route-local browser-shaped fallback pages were introduced.

references.label: NHS App JavaScript API v2
references.borrowed: Host-owned navigation and storage actions remain distinct from route design convergence.
references.rejected: The design bundle does not bundle or simulate the NHS App JS API.

references.label: NHS accessibility content guidance
references.borrowed: Meaningful headings, labels, and error-summary behavior informed the semantic and copy registries.
references.rejected: No generic compliance banner or accessibility widget was added.

references.label: NHS content guide
references.borrowed: Plain-English utility copy shaped the microcopy normalizer.
references.rejected: Marketing-style copy was not added to patient route surfaces.

references.label: WAI-ARIA APG main landmark
references.borrowed: Route-owned main landmarks stay intact while the wrapper publishes a secondary design contract.
references.rejected: The provider does not create another main landmark.

references.label: WAI-ARIA APG tabs
references.borrowed: Tabbed route navigation remains labelled and order-preserving.
references.rejected: No custom tab keyboard implementation was added over existing route buttons.

references.label: Playwright ARIA snapshots
references.borrowed: ARIA snapshots verify design-bundle semantics in browser context.
references.rejected: Snapshots are not treated as the only proof; targeted assertions validate contract attributes.

references.label: Playwright emulation
references.borrowed: Reduced-motion and narrow viewport contexts are used in visual proof.
references.rejected: No desktop-only proof path is accepted.

references.label: Playwright visual comparisons
references.borrowed: Screenshots are used as narrow-width baselines for route family convergence.
references.rejected: Pixel diffs are not used as a substitute for semantic and fallback assertions.

references.label: Linear UI redesign
references.borrowed: Reduced noise, fewer decorative elements, and stronger hierarchy guided the invisible wrapper approach.
references.rejected: No new decorative dashboard chrome was added.

references.label: Next.js layouts and pages
references.borrowed: Persistent layout continuity informed the provider/wrapper structure.
references.rejected: No framework migration or routing change was introduced.

references.label: Carbon data table style
references.borrowed: Compact row and column guidance shaped fallback table grammar.
references.rejected: No Carbon dependency or brand styling was imported.
